questions.question: Describe the Banker's algorithm for deadlock detection and recovery. Explain how it works and its advantages.

A process P1 requests 2 units of Resource R1 and 3 units of Resource R2. Process P2 requests 1 unit of Resource R1 and 2 units of Resource R2. Process P3 requests 2 units of Resource R1 and 1 unit of Resource R2. Process P4 requests 3 units of Resource R1 and 2 units of Resource R2. Which process will deadlock and why?

A deadlock situation occurs when Process P1 is holding Resource R1 and waiting for Resource R2, while Process P2 is holding Resource R2 and waiting for Resource R1. Describe the steps involved in recovering from this deadlock situation using the 'preemption' method.
